title: cyperus odoratus subsp. transcaucasicus
intro: Cyperus odoratus subsp. transcaucasicus, also known as the fragrant papyrus sedge, is a subspecies of sedge plant that belongs to the Cyperaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cyperus odoratus subsp. transcaucasicus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
